subject: [SDC] Support setting SDC-BE configuration at deployment time
commit_message:
[SDC] Support setting SDC-BE configuration at deployment time

This would allow us to specify a SDC configuration applied at
deployment time to all instances of SDC without the need to fork
the design time configuration or update configurations at runtime

Signed-off-by: Vasyl Razinkov <vasyl.razinkov@est.tech>
Change-Id: I1167fcccbd12aef10ceeb669027726312b928b59
Issue-ID: SDC-3076
Signed-off-by: Sylvain Desbureaux <sylvain.desbureaux@orange.com>
Signed-off-by: ChrisC <christophe.closset@intl.att.com>

I agree that it's a good direction but before we can merge that mentioned password issues has to be resolved as we don't allow new passwords in config files and from oom perspective this whole file is new

It's definitely better then the pure config file but it's still not there;) The ultimate solution would be to use common secret template for this + init container that will substitute references to environment variable with their real values.

You can find some easy example in dgbuilder:

https://github.com/onap/oom/blob/master/kubernetes/common/dgbuilder/values.yaml (see secrets:)

https://github.com/onap/oom/blob/master/kubernetes/common/dgbuilder/templates/deployment.yaml (checkout the initContainer)

> > > > I agree that it's a good direction but before we can merge that mentioned password issues has to be resolved as we don't allow new passwords in config files and from oom perspective this whole file is new
> > > 
> > > @Krzysztof Opasiak, sure, I see your point, but as you can see from related JIRA, whole point of this commit is to have all configurations needed to run SDC-BE on deployment time, not sure how it can be done without exposing username/password and all others. 
> > > Can you check my latest patch, is this could be acceptable solution? If yes, I'll change all others.
> > 
> > It's definitely better then the pure config file but it's still not there;) The ultimate solution would be to use common secret template for this + init container that will substitute references to environment variable with their real values.
> > 
> > You can find some easy example in dgbuilder:
> > 
> > https://github.com/onap/oom/blob/master/kubernetes/common/dgbuilder/values.yaml (see secrets:)
> > 
> > https://github.com/onap/oom/blob/master/kubernetes/common/dgbuilder/templates/deployment.yaml (checkout the initContainer)
> 
> Right, but all "plain-text" passwords still present in values.yaml, so does not make big difference, IMPO.

Well not really. It makes a difference because:
1) with common secret template those passwords are stored in secrets not in config map which prevents kubernetes from storing them in logs and leaking them in many other places. You can also setup encryption at rest for your cluster which would encrypt all secrets before storing them in etcd
2) when you already use common secret template it's way easier for us to enable per deployment password generation like we did for mariadb-galera in this release. In that case your passwords are no longer hardcoded but generated based on masterPassword for every deployment

Patch Set 10: Code-Review-1

OK so you have to give a right commit message (see https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/OOM+Guilin+release+plan for details)


Also, SDC is not using automatic retrieval of certificates and it must be the first patch for Guilin
So this patch will not be merged before another patch is pushed (and merged) around automatic retrieval of certificates

You can look at NBI as a simple example for that
